focus: primary workflow contract completeness, overflow separation, path/tool claim fidelity, and validator results

findingDetails.title: Root template still permits PARTIAL verdicts that the workflow forbids
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md limits statuses to PASS, FAIL, and SKIP, while the copied root scaffold defines PARTIAL scenario and feature verdicts.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove PARTIAL from the scaffold or amend the primary workflow to define it consistently.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Primary workflow directs users to copy this root scaffold, so the mismatch propagates into generated playbooks.

findingDetails.title: Template placeholder markdown links fail the required relative-path resolution check
findingDetails.evidence: Scoped link resolution found six missing links, all brace-placeholder markdown links in the root template.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use code spans, escaped examples, or real in-repo example links for placeholders; generate concrete links only after substitution.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped link check covered SKILL.md, README.md, all references, and both asset templates; all misses came from root-template placeholders.

findingDetails.title: Root template duplicates the per-feature scaffold that the packet also ships as a separate template
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md directs per-feature files to use the snippet template, but the root template embeds a second full per-feature scaffold.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace the embedded scaffold with a backlink to the dedicated snippet template or mark one source non-authoritative.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The duplicated scaffold appears in the root template and the dedicated snippet template only.

noveltyJustification: First valid iteration after first-run initialization; all three findings are new and evidence-backed against the reviewed target.
traceabilityChecks: SKILL.md is broadly self-sufficient but contradicts active root scaffold on verdict statuses.
README/references are overflow rather than primary workflow duplication.
Shared script names and validator flags verified.
Relative links resolve except six root-template placeholder links.
All target docs validate with exit code 0; asset templates emit non-blocking warnings.
edgeCases: First-run state initialized under bound packet root by explicit dispatch authorization.
Brace-placeholder template links may be intentional post-copy placeholders but fail source-template link resolution.
Asset validator warnings are non-blocking under validate_document.py.

findingDetails.title: README quick start partially duplicates the authoring workflow instead of staying a pure route map
findingDetails.evidence: README has ordered quick-start steps overlapping the SKILL.md authoring workflow, creating a second procedural source that can drift.
findingDetails.recommendation: Point to SKILL.md §3 for the ordered workflow and keep README to route-map wayfinding for templates, references, and validators.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The duplication is limited to README quick-start steps and SKILL.md authoring workflow; references remain overflow.

findingDetails.title: Examples reference overstates one shipped playbook as current while the template marks it older
findingDetails.evidence: examples.md says listed implementations are current-contract packages, but the root template calls system-code-graph an older package shape.
findingDetails.recommendation: Split current canonical examples from legacy/migration examples or align both files on the same status.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The contradiction is between examples.md and the root template examples list; no other reference file makes the same current-contract claim.

noveltyJustification: Two new P2 maintainability findings add low-severity signal against cumulative active finding weight P1=2 and P2=3; existing P1/P2 findings were confirmed but not counted as new.

findingDetails.title: Root template still tells per-feature prompts to follow RCAF even though the workflow defaults to natural-human voice
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md defaults Prompt to natural-human voice and reserves RCAF for AI-orchestrator actors, while the root template says per-feature files should use Role -> Context -> Action -> Format wording.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace the root-template line with the current conditional prompt-voice rule.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared primary workflow prompt rule, dedicated snippet template, and root template per-feature guidance; only root template line has unconditional RCAF-style wording.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 correctness finding adds low-severity signal against cumulative active weight P1=2 and P2=4; prior active findings were carried forward but not counted as new.
